title: data_manifest
description: Per-preview manifest of every data point used in an analysis (SEC filings, manual entry, Yahoo Finance, etc.). Each entry records the source, ticker, fiscal period, metric name, value, and pulled_at timestamp. The audit gate uses this to enforce freshness (>24h is stale) and coverage (every key_metric must have an entry).

ticker.description: Uppercase ticker symbol; allows dot/dash for dual-class (BRK.A) and ADR conventions.

created_at.description: ISO-8601 UTC timestamp of manifest creation.

updated_at.description: ISO-8601 UTC timestamp of most recent append_entry call.

source_id.description: Stable unique identifier for this entry. Convention: '{tool_name}_{metric}_{period}_{pulled_at_iso}' or any UUID. Used to deduplicate when the runner re-runs.

tool_name.description: Source that produced this datum. Examples: sec_filing_10K, sec_filing_10Q, sec_filing_8K, yahoo_finance_prices, manual_consensus, sell_side_note. Free string but conventionally namespace-prefixed.

period.description: Fiscal period this datum is for. Canonical form C{q}Q{yy} or null for period-agnostic data (e.g., calendar event date, balance sheet snapshot at a specific date).

metric.description: Metric name. Convention: snake_case, optional period suffix when the metric is period-specific. Examples: consensus_revenue, consensus_eps_C1Q26, calendar_event_date, historical_actual_revenue_C1Q25.

value.description: The actual datum. Numbers preferred for scalar metrics; structured objects/arrays accepted for compound values (e.g., a calendar event with multiple fields). The audit's freshness check does not interpret value; the figures-coverage check does, and prefers numeric primitives where possible.

unit.description: Unit string for numeric values. Examples: $mm, $bn, %, bps, count. Null for non-numeric or already-encoded units.

pulled_at.description: ISO-8601 UTC timestamp of when the data point was obtained. Used by the freshness gate.

source_url.description: Optional URL or filing-reference for primary sources (e.g., SEC filing accession number or EDGAR URL).
